title: Consultation: Draft guidance on sampling and mitigation measures for controlling corrosion - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Draft guidance on sampling and mitigation measures for controlling corrosion

## Current status: Closed
Opened on December 16, 2022, and will close to new input on February 15, 2023.
Health Canada has developed a guidance document to provide information on sampling and the mitigation measures needed to control corrosion in drinking water systems. We are looking for comments from regulatory authorities, decision makers and the public on the draft guidance.

## Join in: How to participate
Send us an email Send an email to water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca with your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.

## Who is the focus of this engagement
Health Canada  welcomes input from the following:
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations
- members of the general public

## Key questions for discussion
We are looking for suggestions and comments on:
- scientifically supported sampling approaches and mitigation measures to control corrosion in drinking water distribution systems
We will use the information we gather from this consultation to prepare and update the Guidance on controlling corrosion in drinking water systems .
Water and Air Quality Bureau Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON  K1A 0K9 Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
